ontology.foodex2_description: Mango, fresh — used as closest parent for green mango salad dish; no composite Thai salad code available in FoodEx2 matrix.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Thai green mango salad as a composite dish. A00LC (Mango, fresh) is the closest single-ingredient anchor. A composite dish code would require facet descriptors not available in this context.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Thai green mango salad or vegetarian mango salad as a prepared dish.

preparation_method: dressed with lime juice and fish-sauce-free dressing (vegetarian)
main_ingredients: vegetarian fish sauce or soy sauce
dried shrimp substitute (omitted in vegetarian version)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard restaurant serving of Thai green mango salad is approximately 200 g, typically presented as one plate or bowl containing shredded green mango, peanuts, chilli, and dressing.

nutrient_source.title: Green Mango Salad (Yam Mamuang) — nutrient profile estimated from USDA FoodData Central component foods: raw mango (FDC ID 169910), lime juice (FDC ID 167747), roasted peanuts (FDC ID 172430), palm sugar, shallots, chilli, and soy sauce, weighted to a representative recipe proportion.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry for Thai green mango salad as a composite dish was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component ingredients using a representative recipe: 55% shredded green mango, 10% roasted peanuts, 8% shallots, 5% chilli, 5% lime juice, 5% soy sauce / vegetarian fish sauce, 5% palm sugar, 7% coriander and herbs. Values are per 100 g of the assembled salad. Confidence is medium due to recipe-level estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for raw mango (GI ~51) used as the primary analogous anchor for green (unripe) mango salad.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Thai green mango salad exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Green (unripe) mango has a lower sugar content than ripe mango and a higher organic acid content, which suppresses glycemic response. The GI of ripe mango is reported at approximately 51 (low GI) in the University of Sydney database and in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). Unripe green mango is expected to have a GI equal to or lower than ripe mango due to higher resistant starch and lower free sugar content; GI of 51 is therefore a conservative upper-bound estimate for the salad. Peanuts and lime juice in the dressing further lower the overall meal GI. GL per 200 g serving = (51 × 10.8 × 2) / 100 ≈ 11.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce or vegetarian fish sauce
hypertension.guidance: A 200 g serving contributes approximately 620 mg sodium, which represents about 27% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sauce preparation or limit portion size to manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~51) and moderate carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Thai green mango salad has a low estimated GI of approximately 51 and provides about 21.6 g of carbohydrate per 200 g serving, making it a suitable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when palm sugar in the dressing is minimised; the dietary fibre (3.6 g per serving) and peanut fat further attenuate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, cholesterol-free, rich in vitamin C and potassium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This vegetarian salad is cholesterol-free, low in saturated fat (1.2 g per serving), and provides approximately 370 mg potassium and 36 mg vitamin C per 200 g serving, supporting vascular health; the primary cardiovascular caution is the moderate sodium load from the dressing, which should be reduced for patients with established cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Thai Green Mango Salad (Yam Mamuang, ยำมะม่วง) is a vibrant, refreshing Thai salad made from shredded unripe (green) mango tossed with a tangy-sweet-spicy dressing of lime juice, palm sugar, chilli, and vegetarian fish sauce or soy sauce, garnished with roasted peanuts, shallots, and fresh coriander. In its vegetarian form, dried shrimp is omitted and replaced with additional herbs or toasted sesame. The texture is crisp and crunchy from the firm unripe mango and peanuts, with a bold flavour profile that balances sour, sweet, salty, and spicy notes characteristic of Thai cuisine. Nutritionally, a 200 g serving provides approximately 164 kcal, 4.8 g protein, 7.6 g fat, and 21.6 g carbohydrate, with a low estimated glycemic index of 51, making it suitable for blood glucose management. The dish is rich in vitamin C (36 mg per serving) and provides useful amounts of potassium and dietary fibre. The primary nutritional concern is sodium from the dressing (approximately 620 mg per serving), which is relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. This dish is commonly served as a starter or side dish in Thai restaurants across Singapore and Southeast Asia, and is popular as a light, low-calorie option. It is naturally vegetarian, dairy-free, and halal-friendly when prepared without fish sauce.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry for Thai green mango salad as a composite dish was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component ingredients using a representative recipe proportion. GI is estimated from the closest analogous approved-source entry (ripe mango, GI 51, University of Sydney GI Database). Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is high quality and the recipe proportions are well-established for this dish type.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are recipe-estimated from USDA FoodData Central component foods; a direct laboratory analysis or HPB/FAO composite dish entry would upgrade confidence to high. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this dish contains no hydrogenated fats; all ingredients are natural plant foods. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a fully plant-based vegetarian dish with no animal-derived ingredients. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based dish with no animal products; B12 is naturally zero in plant foods. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as no vitamin D-containing ingredients (fatty fish, eggs, fortified dairy) are present in this dish. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g (1.7), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.2), potassium_mg (185.0), calcium_mg (22.0), iron_mg (0.5), vitamin_c_mg (18.0), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (28.0), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.05), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.04), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (0.9), vitamin_b6_mg (0.1), folate_mcg_dfe (22.0), vitamin_e_mg (1.1), phosphorus_mg (48.0), magnesium_mg (18.0), zinc_mg (0.3), selenium_mcg (1.2), water_g (78.5) are all estimated from USDA FoodData Central component ingredient data. 7. GI of 51 is a conservative upper-bound estimate based on ripe mango GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; actual GI of green mango salad may be lower. 8. Sodium content is highly variable depending on the amount of soy sauce or vegetarian fish sauce used; the value of 310 mg per 100 g reflects a moderate dressing quantity. 9. is_vegan set to false because some restaurant preparations may include honey in the dressing; is_vegetarian is true for the declared vegetarian version. 10. The food_name has been normalised from the input menu string '607D Vegetarian Mango Salad 青芒果沙拉' to the canonical English form 'Thai Green Mango Salad (Vegetarian)'; the original menu code 607D and Chinese name 青芒果沙拉 are preserved in aliases. 11. Q8 fix (iteration 3): verified all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases. 'Yam Mamuang (Mangsawirat)' added to flat aliases to match romanized entry in aliases_by_language.
